title: Ranking Keys, Stable Sorts, Ties, and Top-K Reports · Behavior Case: Stable Sort
summary: Equal-frequency words appear in hash-dependent order, reverse sorting reverses both count and token unexpectedly, top-k truncation happens before ranking, and repeated runs produce noisy diffs. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the stable sort increment in deterministic ranked-frequency report. Learners progre…
objectives: Use and test Python stable sorting when earlier order is intentionally retained within equal keys.
Express mixed ascending and descending order with an explicit key or stable multi-pass sort and trace tuple comparison.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-stable-sort — Use and test Python stable sorting when earlier order is intentionally retained within equal keys.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-ranking-determinism-5
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-ranking-determinism-5.
# Case 9036: a digital-humanities corpus revision.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def worked_bookbot_ranking_determinism_5(eligible_words: int = 9, ranked_words: int = 9, top_k: int = 5, ties_resolved: bool = True, repeated_output_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "corpusworked5",
        "policy5dekrow",
        "revision9036",
        "commandworked59036",
        "report5dekrow9036",
        "test9036worked5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if eligible_words < 0 or ranked_words < 0:
        return False, ranked_words, "rank-shape"
    if ranked_words != eligible_words:
        return False, ranked_words, "rank-coverage"
    if top_k < 1 or top_k > ranked_words:
        return False, top_k, "top-k"
    if not ties_resolved or not repeated_output_equal:
        return False, top_k, "unstable-ranking"
    assert top_k <= ranked_words
    return True, top_k, "deterministic-top-k"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-stable-sort-ln8801
# Competency: bookbot-stable-sort.
# Case 8036: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def evidence_bookbot_stable_sort_ln8801(eligible_words: int = 9, ranked_words: int = 9, top_k: int = 5, ties_resolved: bool = True, repeated_output_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_ln8801 = "ln8801-1088nl-8036"
    assert evidence_variant_ln8801
    evidence_axes_ln8801 = (
        "corpusln8801",
        "policy1088nl",
        "revision8036",
        "commandln88018036",
        "report1088nl8036",
        "test8036ln8801",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_ln8801)) == 6
    if eligible_words < 0 or ranked_words < 0:
        return False, ranked_words, "rank-shape"
    if ranked_words != eligible_words:
        return False, ranked_words, "rank-coverage"
    if top_k < 1 or top_k > ranked_words:
        return False, top_k, "top-k"
    if not ties_resolved or not repeated_output_equal:
        return False, top_k, "unstable-ranking"
    assert top_k <= ranked_words
    return True, top_k, "deterministic-top-k"

Build deterministic Python evidence to rank all eligible words with explicit ties and reproducible top-k output; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use and test Python stable sorting when earlier order is intentionally retained within equal keys.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Stable Sort model · Theory Stable Sort, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Stable sorting means equal items may be returned in arbitrary order.
